info.name: Profile – Username (GET /username/available + PATCH /me/username)
info.description:
E2E tests for GET /api/v1/profile/username/available and PATCH /api/v1/profile/me/username.

Folder execution order (orchestrated by make e2e-username):

Invocation 1 (single run, --delay-request 1):
  1. setup              — register gmail_test_email, fetch OTP, verify email, login → stores usrn_access_token
  2. happy-path         — GET available=true; PATCH happy path to usrn_happypath; GET available=false; GET /me verify DB write
  3. failures           — seed second user with taken username; same_username 422; username_taken 409; GET empty param 422
  4. auth-failures      — missing Authorization → 401; tampered token → 401
  5. validation         — body > 1 MiB → 413; malformed JSON → 422
  6. rate-limiting-uchg — registers dedicated +usrnrl user (fresh uchg:usr: bucket);
                          5 warmup PATCHes (each → 422 validation_error, slot consumed) → 6th → 429
                          NOTE: runs in same invocation so usrn_rl_access_token is available.

Invocation 2 (after Redis flush):
  7. rate-limiting-unav — 20 warmup GETs (public, IP-keyed) → 21st → 429

Rate-limiter design notes:
  uchg:usr: is user-keyed (PATCH /me/username, inside JWTAuth group). The failures and validation folders
  consume 4 of the 5 uchg:usr: slots for usrn_access_token before rate-limiting-uchg runs. To guarantee
  a fresh burst=5 bucket, rate-limiting-uchg registers a dedicated gmail_test_email+usrnrl alias user
  (usrn_rl_access_token) — the same pattern used by rate-limiting-spw in set-password.json.
  Gmail delivers +usrnrl mail to the same inbox; the OTP fetch targets the alias with a to: filter.
  Warmup bodies send { "username": "" } to trigger 422 validation_error after the rate limiter —
  this consumes a slot without needing the RL user to have an existing username.

  unav:ip: is IP-keyed (GET /username/available, public endpoint). It runs in a separate invocation
  after a Redis flush. Its folder prerequest pins _xff to 127.0.0.1 so the IP bucket starts empty.

Variable prefix: usrn_

Notes on what is NOT covered here and why:
  - Individual validation variants (too short, too long, invalid charset, leading/trailing/consecutive
    underscores): covered by handler_test.go TestHandler_Available and TestHandler_UpdateUsername.
  - Audit row event type and DB-level metadata content: covered by store_test.go T-36.
  - Same-username guard inside the DB transaction: covered by store_test.go T-35.
  - Unique-violation race (TOCTOU): covered by store_test.go T-34.
  - context.WithoutCancel absence: not applicable per Stage 0 §5 (not required).
  - QuerierProxy error injection: covered by store_test.go T-37, T-38.
  - Tampered JWT (T-30): JWT middleware responsibility; confirmed on wire via auth-failures folder.

All test users use gmail_test_email or @e2e.test addresses so `make _e2e-db-clean` can wipe them.

exec: // Increment the request sequence counter and derive a unique X-Forwarded-For IP
// for every request so non-RL folders never exhaust any rate-limit bucket.

item.name: setup
item.description: Registers the gmail_test_email user, fetches the verification OTP from Gmail, verifies the email, and logs in. Stores usrn_access_token for use by subsequent folders.

exec: console.error('[ERROR] gmail_test_email not set in environment — add it to environment.json.');
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for verification email to arrive in Gmail...');
console.log('[INFO] Done waiting. Querying Gmail API...');
const query = encodeURIComponent('subject:"Verify your" to:' + usrnEmail + ' newer_than:1h');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired — refresh at https://developers.google.com/oauthplayground'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No matching verification email found in Gmail.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode verification email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No 6-digit OTP found in verification email.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Verification OTP stored in usrn_vfy_code:', match[1]);
console.error('[ERROR] usrn_vfy_code is empty — Gmail fetch in register step did not complete.');
console.error('        Check: gmail_access_token is set, token not expired, --delay-request >= 8000.');
console.log('[INFO] Access token stored in usrn_access_token.');

item.name: happy-path
item.description:
Covers T-01 (GET available=true), the PATCH happy path (T-15), T-02 (GET available=false after PATCH), and a follow-up GET /me to confirm the DB write.

Order: GET available=true → PATCH to usrn_happypath → GET available=false (usrn_happypath is now taken) → GET /me verify.

NOT covered here:
  - store error → 500 (T-10): covered by handler_test.go.
  - audit row content (T-36): covered by store_test.go.

exec: // T-01 — username that no test user holds; should be available.
// Confirms the public endpoint returns 200 with available=true on the wire.
// Anti-enumeration (D-03): endpoint always returns 200, never 404 or 409.
// T-15 — authenticated user changes to a fresh, available username.
// Confirms the full wire: JWT auth → rate limiter → handler → service → DB → 200.
// NOT covered here: audit row content (T-36) — covered by store_test.go.
// T-02 — username held by the setup user (just set via PATCH above); should be taken.
// Confirms the availability check correctly reports taken after a successful PATCH.
// Anti-enumeration (D-03): no 404 or 409 even when the username is taken.
// Wire-level confirmation that the PATCH actually persisted to the DB.
// This is not a duplicate of T-15 — that test checks the PATCH response shape.
// This test checks that a subsequent GET /me reflects the new username.

item.name: failures
item.description:
Domain-level error cases that require real HTTP round-trips to confirm wiring: same_username (T-23), username_taken (T-24), and a representative GET validation error (T-03).

The first request seeds a second @e2e.test user with username=usrntakenuser so the username_taken 409 test has a real conflict in the DB. No Gmail OTP is needed for this user — registration inserts the row immediately regardless of email verification status, which is all CheckUsernameAvailable queries.

NOT covered here:
  - Other 422 validation variants (T-16–T-22): covered by handler_test.go.
  - Individual GET validation variants (T-04–T-09): covered by handler_test.go.

exec: // Seed a second user in the DB who holds username=usrntakenuser.
// Registration inserts the users row immediately (email_verified=false is fine).
// CheckUsernameAvailable (SELECT EXISTS WHERE username=...) will find this row,
// causing the subsequent PATCH by the main usrn user to return 409 username_taken.
// 409 is also acceptable here if a prior run left this user in the DB.
// T-23 — usrn user tries to PATCH to their current username (usrn_happypath).
// After normalisation both values are equal → 422 same_username.
// Confirms: rate limiter passes → service same-username guard fires → handler maps → wire.
// NOTE: this request consumes one uchg:usr: slot for usrn_access_token.
// T-24 — usrn user tries to claim usrntakenuser which belongs to the seeded user.
// UpdateUsernameTx: SELECT FOR UPDATE → unique violation on idx_users_username → ErrUsernameTaken.
// NOTE: this request consumes one uchg:usr: slot for usrn_access_token.
// T-03 — empty username query param → 422 validation_error.
// Confirms the validation wiring fires on the wire for the public GET endpoint.
// Other GET validation variants (T-04–T-09) are covered by handler_test.go.

item.name: auth-failures
item.description:
Verifies that the JWT auth middleware correctly guards PATCH /me/username.
Covers T-29 (missing Authorization) and T-30 (tampered token).
Neither request reaches the rate limiter — JWT middleware fires first and returns 401.

exec: // T-29 — no Authorization header at all.
// JWTAuth middleware must reject this before the rate limiter or handler body runs.
// mustUserID guard is also exercised by this missing-header case.
// NOTE: no uchg:usr: slot consumed — JWT rejection fires before rate limiter.
// T-30 — JWT with tampered ECDSA signature.
// JWTAuth middleware validates the signature and must reject this with 401.
// This is JWT middleware responsibility — confirmed on wire here.
// NOTE: no uchg:usr: slot consumed — JWT rejection fires before rate limiter.

item.name: validation
item.description:
Infrastructure-level request validation: body size cap (T-31) and malformed JSON (T-32).
Both use usrn_access_token since JWTAuth fires before body decoding.

NOTE: Both requests pass the JWT middleware and hit the rate limiter — they each consume
one uchg:usr: slot from usrn_access_token. Combined with the 2 slots consumed in failures,
usrn_access_token's burst=5 bucket is fully exhausted before rate-limiting-uchg runs.
rate-limiting-uchg therefore uses a dedicated second user (usrn_rl_access_token) with
its own fresh burst=5 bucket.

exec: // Pad the body past the 1 MiB MaxBytesReader limit set in handler.UpdateUsername.
// Rate limiter fires first (consuming one uchg:usr: slot), then MaxBytesReader
// fires during json.Decoder.Decode → respond.DecodeJSON maps to 413.
// Rate limiter fires first (consuming one uchg:usr: slot), then decoder fails → 400.

item.name: rate-limiting-uchg
item.description:
Verifies the uchg:usr: per-user rate limiter for PATCH /me/username (burst=5, rate=5/10 min).

Why a dedicated user (usrn_rl_access_token):
  The uchg:usr: limiter is user-keyed. The failures folder (2 PATCHes) and validation folder
  (2 PATCHes) each use usrn_access_token, exhausting all 5 slots of that user's bucket before
  this folder runs. To guarantee a fresh burst=5 bucket, this folder registers and logs in as a
  separate gmail_test_email+usrnrl alias user (usrn_rl_access_token). Gmail delivers +usrnrl
  mail to the same inbox; the OTP fetch targets the alias with a to: filter.

Warmup strategy:
  Warmup bodies send { "username": "" } → validation_error 422 after the rate limiter.
  No need for the RL user to have an existing username — empty username triggers validation
  immediately after the limiter passes it through.

Sequence:
  rl-setup 1–3: register +usrnrl alias → verify email (Gmail OTP) → login (store usrn_rl_access_token)
  warmup  1–5:  not 429 (slots 1–5 consumed; each returns 422 validation_error)
  6th request → 429 too_many_requests (bucket exhausted)

Retry-After = ceil(1 / (5/600)) = ceil(120) = 120 seconds.

Covers T-39: 6th request from same user within 10-minute window → 429 (uchg:usr:, burst=5).

exec: // Pin _xff to 127.0.0.1 for all requests in this folder.
// The uchg:usr: limiter keys on user ID extracted from the JWT, not IP.
// Pinning IP is a safety measure so this folder never pollutes other buckets.
// (The collection prerequest still increments _req_seq but _xff is overridden here.)
// Derive the +usrnrl Gmail alias from gmail_test_email.
// Gmail delivers user+usrnrl@gmail.com to the same inbox as user@gmail.com.
// Fetch the verification OTP for the +usrnrl alias specifically.
// Using to: filter so we get the right email when both users registered in the same hour.
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for +usrnrl verification email...');
const query = encodeURIComponent('subject:"Verify your" to:' + rlEmail + ' newer_than:1h');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No +usrnrl verification email found.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode +usrnrl email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No OTP found in +usrnrl email.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] +usrnrl OTP stored in usrn_rl_vfy_code:', match[1]);
console.error('[ERROR] usrn_rl_vfy_code is empty — Gmail fetch in rl-setup 1/3 did not complete.');
console.log('[INFO] RL access token stored in usrn_rl_access_token.');
// Uses usrn_rl_access_token (fresh bucket, no prior PATCH calls for this user).
// Empty username → rate limiter fires (slot 1 consumed) → 422 validation_error.

item.name: warmup 5/5 — consume uchg:usr: slot (→ not 429, last allowed)

exec: pm.test('not rate-limited yet (uchg:usr: slot 5 — last allowed)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.not.eql(429));
// T-39 — burst=5 exhausted. The rate limiter fires before the handler.
// NOT covered here: exact Retry-After value — covered by rate-limiter unit tests.

item.name: rate-limiting-unav
item.description:
Verifies the unav:ip: per-IP rate limiter for GET /username/available (burst=20, rate=20/1 min).

This folder runs in a SEPARATE Newman invocation after _e2e-kv-clean so the 127.0.0.1
IP bucket starts empty. The folder prerequest pins _xff to 127.0.0.1 for all requests
so they all land in the same bucket.

Sequence:
  warmup 1–25: GET → 200 (slots 1–20 consumed + 5 drain requests to absorb token refill; must not return 429)
  26th request → 429 too_many_requests (bucket exhausted)

Why 25 warmup requests instead of 20:
  The unav:ip: limiter is a token bucket (rate=20/60≈0.333 tok/sec). During the warmup
  phase Redis response latency means the 20 requests can take 2–6 seconds total, refilling
  up to ~2 tokens. Without extra drain requests the 21st fires into a non-empty bucket and
  returns 200 instead of 429, causing an intermittent flaky failure. Sending 5 additional
  drain requests (slots 21–25) absorbs up to ~15 seconds of refill (5/0.333=15s), making
  the test deterministic on any machine/network speed.

Covers T-14: first request beyond burst+refill from same IP within 1-minute window → 429 (unav:ip:, burst=20).

exec: // Pin _xff to 127.0.0.1 for all requests so they land in the same IP bucket.
// The _e2e-kv-clean before this invocation guarantees the bucket starts empty.
// The global prerequest still increments _req_seq but _xff is overridden here.
// Drain — no assertion, see slot 21 comment.
// Drain — no assertion, see slot 21 comment.
// Drain — no assertion, see slot 21 comment.

item.name: warmup 25/25 — drain refill tokens (silent, bucket now reliably empty)

exec: // Drain — no assertion. After this the bucket is reliably empty and the 26th request must 429.
// T-14 — burst=20 exhausted. The rate limiter fires and returns 429.
// NOT covered here: exact Retry-After value — covered by rate-limiter unit tests.

variable.description: Auto-incremented counter used by the collection prerequest to generate unique X-Forwarded-For IPs.
Current X-Forwarded-For value. Set to a unique 10.x.x.x IP by the collection prerequest for every request. Overridden to 127.0.0.1 by rate-limiting folder prerequest scripts.
Set from gmail_test_email in setup/register prerequest.
Password for usrn_email (P@ssw0rd1!). Set in setup/register prerequest.
Verification OTP for usrn_email fetched from Gmail by setup/register test script.
Access token for usrn_email from setup/login. Used by happy-path, failures, auth-failures, and validation folders.
Current username of the usrn user. Set to 'usrn_happypath' after the happy-path PATCH. Used in same_username failures test.
gmail_test_email+usrnrl alias. Derived and set in rate-limiting-uchg rl-setup 1/3 prerequest. Fresh uchg:usr: bucket — not used by any other folder.
Password for usrn_rl_email (RlUsrnP@ss1!). Set in rate-limiting-uchg rl-setup 1/3 prerequest.
Verification OTP for usrn_rl_email fetched from Gmail by rl-setup 1/3 test script (to: filter targets the +usrnrl alias).
Access token for usrn_rl_email from rl-setup 3/3. Used exclusively by rate-limiting-uchg warmups and the 6th-request trigger. Guarantees a fresh burst=5 bucket.
